files.note: NEW FILE (written to disk at C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs). Compile-checked clean with stable rustc (-D warnings, deny(missing_docs)) against a stub of the spec'd shared layer `crate::mmu::PageTable512(pub [u64;512])` — the ONLY symbol it imports from the shared typed layer, exactly as quoted in the M3 contract. Cross-file expectations for the integrator: (1) crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs must exist with `#[repr(C, align(4096))] pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64;512]);` and lib.rs must declare `mod mmu;` (or `pub mod mmu;`); (2) crates/tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs must add `mmu_init, mmu_selftest` to BOTH per-arch re-export lists so lib.rs can wrap them. Design: CR3->PML4[0] walk finds the boot PDPT at runtime (no extern symbol coupling to boot.rs); PDPT[1] (TEST_VA=0x4000_0000 -> indexes PML4=0/PDPT=1/PD=0/PT=0) is checked VACANT fail-closed before splicing; child tables built bottom-up and published last; remap = rewrite PT[0] to FRAME_B + invlpg (REQUIRED per SDM 4.10.4.2; the first-map invlpg is optional per 4.10.4.3 and kept as belt-and-suspenders); all table/frame statics are zero-init `.bss` Cell4K(UnsafeCell<PageTable512>) accessed only via raw pointers + volatile. VERIFIED register bits (WebFetch; osdev wiki was Cloudflare-403 so verified against Linux v6.6 headers which transcribe Intel SDM Table 4-15 / Vol 4 Table 2-2): msr-index.h `#define MSR_EFER 0xc0000080`, `#define _EFER_NX 11`, `#define MSR_IA32_CR_PAT 0x00000277`; pgtable_types.h `_PAGE_BIT_PRESENT 0`, `_PAGE_BIT_RW 1`, `_PAGE_BIT_PSE 7`, `_PAGE_BIT_NX 63 /* No execute: only valid after cpuid check */`; processor-flags.h `#define X86_CR4_PGE_BIT 7`. INVLPG semantics cited from Intel SDM Vol 2A (invalidates TLB entries for the page containing the operand) and Vol 3A §4.10.4.2/§4.10.4.3 (present-entry change requires invalidation; 0->1 P transition does not). Every asm! wrapper carries the repo's 3-line (a) PRE/POST (b) ABI (c) Tested-by header; write_cr3 and cr4_pge_toggle are provided per contract but #[allow(dead_code)] (unused by the selftest, documented why).

UPDATED FILE (full file; written to disk at C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mod.rs). Minimally invasive: every M0/M1/M2 module declaration, re-export (serial_init, serial_write_byte, breakpoint, ctx_switch, task_stack_init), install_traps and halt are preserved byte-for-byte. Three additions only: (1) an M3 paragraph appended to the module doc header; (2) `pub mod mmu;` after `pub mod sched;`; (3) `pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};` after the sched re-export, so arch/mod.rs can re-export them under the same uniform per-arch contract as the aarch64 arm.

NEW (units A6/A7/A9). Cold aarch64 MMU bring-up + M3 self-test. Depends on crate::mmu::PageTable512 (shared typed layer, owned by another agent). Wrappers: msr mair_el1/tcr_el1/ttbr0_el1; sctlr_el1_set_bits = mrs/orr(reg)/msr/isb RMW (0x1005 is not a valid AArch64 logical immediate, hence register form); dsb ishst / dsb ish / isb (deliberately NOT options(nomem) so they remain compiler barriers); tlbi vaae1is with operand VA>>12 (bits[43:0] = VA[55:12], ASID 0). mmu_init: L1[0]=Device-nGnRnE 1GiB block 0x701 (covers PL011 @0x0900_0000), L1[1]=Normal-WB 1GiB block 0x4000_0705 (covers RAM, AF bit10 set on every leaf) -> dsb ishst -> MAIR=0xFF00, TCR=0x2_0099_3519 (T0SZ=25, IRGN0/ORGN0=0b01, SH0=0b11, TG0=4K, T1SZ=25, EPD1=1, IPS=0b010/40-bit), TTBR0=ID_L1 -> isb -> SCTLR|=M|C|I (bits 0,2,12; RMW preserves rest) -> isb. mmu_selftest: TEST_VA=0x8000_0000 (L1[2]/L2[0]/L3[0]); chain built bottom-up, live-root plug last; frame B pre-seeded via identity alias; write MAGIC_A via TEST_VA, verify via identity; full BBM remap (L3[0]=0 -> dsb ishst -> tlbi vaae1is,VA>>12 -> dsb ish -> isb -> new leaf -> dsb ishst -> isb); verify MAGIC_B via TEST_VA + frame A intact. Statics (.bss, zeroed by _start): ID_L1/TEST_L2/TEST_L3 as repr(transparent) UnsafeCell<PageTable512> cells (inherit align 4096), FRAME_A/FRAME_B as repr(C,align(4096)) frames; all table/frame access volatile via raw pointers, no interior references minted. Sources (fetched 2026-06-07; osdev wiki CF-403'd, cross-verified instead): Arm ARM DDI 0487 VMSAv8-64 descriptors D8.3 / BBM D8.16 / TLBI D8.14 / TCR-SCTLR-MAIR D19.2; Linux v6.6 arch/arm64/include/asm/pgtable-hwdef.h (PMD_TYPE_SECT 1<<0, PUD_TYPE_TABLE 3<<0, PTE_TYPE_PAGE 3<<0, PTE_AF 1<<10, PTE_SHARED 3<<8 inner-shareable, PTE_ATTRINDX t<<2, AP[1]=1<<6/AP[2]=1<<7, TCR_T0SZ_OFFSET 0, TCR_IRGN0_WBWA 1<<8, TCR_ORGN0_WBWA 1<<10, TCR_SH0_INNER 3<<12, TCR_TG0_4K 0<<14, TCR_T1SZ_OFFSET 16, TCR_EPD1_SHIFT 23, TCR_IPS_SHIFT 32), sysreg.h (SCTLR_ELx_M/C/I = BIT(0)/BIT(2)/BIT(12), MAIR_ATTR_DEVICE_nGnRnE 0x00, MAIR_ATTR_NORMAL 0xff, MAIR_ATTRIDX attr<<(idx*8)), tlbflush.h (__TLBI_VADDR = addr>>12 masked GENMASK(43,0); __flush_tlb_kernel_pgtable = dsb ishst; tlbi vaae1is; dsb ish; isb); lowenware.com AArch64 MMU Programming (descriptor diagram AF=10/SH=9:8/AP=7:6/INDX=4:2, QEMU virt UART 0x09000000 + load 0x40080000). Host-typechecked clean (rustc --emit=metadata vs PageTable512 stub, 0 warnings); full nightly custom-target build requires the integrator (crate::mmu + lib.rs surface not yet in tree, no nightly on this host).

UPDATED (full file). Adds `mod mmu;` + `pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};` and extends the module doc to cover M3. ALL M0/M1/M2 exports preserved byte-for-byte: serial_init, serial_write_byte, halt, install_traps, breakpoint, ctx_switch, task_stack_init. arch/mod.rs and lib.rs (owned by other agents) must re-export mmu_init/mmu_selftest from here to complete the safe surface called by kernel/src/main.rs.

NEW shared typed page-table layer (M3). Pure safe Rust, zero unsafe, zero hardware touches: PageTable512 (#[repr(C,align(4096))], pub [u64;512]) + Frame4K (4 KiB test frame as 512 u64 words) + the arch-shared entry/index math (level_index/make_entry/entry_addr/entry_is_valid, SHIFT_512G/1G/2M/4K, ENTRY_ADDR_MASK=bits[47:12]). Both arch MMU backends consume it as crate::mmu::*. const-fn constructors => statics land in .bss zeroed (zero entry = not-present/invalid on BOTH arches). base_addr() documents the identity-map assumption under which VA==PA. ENTRY_ADDR_MASK deliberately keeps the portable [47:12] subset: x86 Table 4-15 puts address bits in [51:12], but VMSAv8-64 uses [51:48] for upper attributes (DBM=bit51); all M3 frames sit <4GiB so nothing is lost. Verified: 9-bit/512-entry slicing per Intel SDM Vol 3A 4.5 (Table 4-15) and ARM ARM VMSAv8-64 4KiB granule; bit0=Present/Valid (Linux v6.6 pgtable_types.h _PAGE_BIT_PRESENT=0; pgtable-hwdef.h PTE_VALID=1<<0).

UPDATED from the live file on disk (read this session) — entire M0/M1/M2 API preserved byte-for-byte (serial_*, halt, install_traps, breakpoint, set_trap_hook, TrapInfo/TrapKind/TrapAction, Task/task_create/yield_to/current_task, TaskStack, dispatch_trap). Additions only: header doc gains the M3 bullet block; `mod mmu;` next to `mod arch;`; new M3 section at the end with `pub fn mmu_init()` and `pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool` delegating to arch::mmu_init / arch::mmu_selftest. CROSS-FILE CONTRACT for the arch agents: arch/x86_64/mod.rs and arch/aarch64/mod.rs must each `pub fn mmu_init()` + `pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool` (or re-export from their mmu submodule), and arch/mod.rs must add `mmu_init, mmu_selftest` to BOTH cfg'd `pub use` lists; the shared typed layer is reachable as crate::mmu::{PageTable512, Frame4K, level_index, make_entry, entry_addr, entry_is_valid, SHIFT_*, ENTRY_ADDR_MASK, PAGE_SIZE, ENTRIES}. Doc claims verified: EFER=MSR 0xC0000080 NXE=bit11 + IA32_PAT=0x277 (Linux v6.6 msr-index.h), invlpg semantics (felixcloutier/Intel SDM Vol 2: privileged, flushes all TLB entries for the page containing the operand), SCTLR_EL1 M=bit0 C=bit2 I=bit12 + MAIR Device-nGnRnE=0x00 Normal-WB=0xFF (Linux v6.6 sysreg.h), TLBI operand=VA>>12 (Linux v6.6 tlbflush.h __TLBI_VADDR).

UPDATED from the live file on disk. The M0 hello, M1 trap round-trip and the M2 1000-round ping-pong with all three canaries are kept verbatim; only task A's terminal action changes: instead of halting after printing 'M2: context-switch OK' it sets M2_PASSED and yields BACK to the bootstrap context (handle stashed in new BOOT_TASK_RAW static via tb_hal::current_task() before the tasks are created), so the M3 steps run literally in rust_main AFTER install_traps as the spec requires. rust_main's old fail('bootstrap context resumed unexpectedly') is replaced by a fail-closed M2_PASSED gate, then the exact M3 sequence: 'mmu-test: init' -> mmu_init() -> 'mmu-test: enabled, serial alive' -> mmu_selftest() ? 'M3: mmu OK' : 'M3: FAIL' -> halt(). Crate still contains zero unsafe blocks (only the #[unsafe(no_mangle)] attribute, as before). Cumulative serial order: hello -> M1: traps OK -> M2: context-switch OK -> mmu-test: init -> mmu-test: enabled, serial alive -> M3: mmu OK.

UPDATED from the live file on disk: MARKER -> 'M3: mmu OK' and the header comment retold for M3 (cumulative: M0 hello, M1 traps, M2 ping-pong still print earlier in the same boot). QEMU invocation, accel/KVM probe, timeout and fail-closed grep semantics unchanged.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-x86_64.sh - QEMU launcher + Definition-of-Done check for M3 (x86_64).
#
# Boots the PVH ELF kernel under QEMU 'microvm' (the machine type Firecracker is
# modelled on), wires legacy 16550 COM1 to stdio, and asserts the EXACT marker
# "M3: mmu OK" appears on serial (the MMU map/remap self-test proof; M0's
# hello, M1's trap round-trip and M2's ping-pong still print earlier in the
# same boot, so one run proves every milestone).
# Fail-closed: a wall-clock timeout always bounds the run (the kernel halts
# rather than exiting), and a missing marker is a non-zero exit.
#
# PVH is selected automatically by QEMU from the XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note
# in the ELF (see crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs + kernel/linker/x86_64.ld).
#   refs: https://xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html
#         https://www.qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html
#
# Usage:   scripts/run-x86_64.sh [path/to/kernel-elf]
# Env:     QEMU=...  QEMU_TIMEOUT=<secs>  PROFILE=debug|release
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TARGET="x86_64-tabos-none"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/${TARGET}/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
MARKER='M3: mmu OK'
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"
QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-x86_64}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "error: ${QEMU} not found on PATH (install qemu-system-x86 in WSL2)" >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [[ ! -f "${KERNEL}" ]]; then
  echo "error: kernel image not found: ${KERNEL}" >&2
  echo "build it first, e.g.:" >&2
  echo "  cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target ${REPO_ROOT}/targets/${TARGET}.json" >&2
  exit 2
fi

# Prefer KVM only when /dev/kvm is actually usable; otherwise pure-TCG so this
# runs in any WSL2 / CI box without nested virt.
ACCEL="tcg"
CPU="qemu64"
if [[ -e /dev/kvm && -r /dev/kvm && -w /dev/kvm ]]; then
  ACCEL="kvm"
  CPU="host"
fi

echo ">> qemu=${QEMU} accel=${ACCEL} cpu=${CPU} timeout=${TIMEOUT_SECS}s" >&2
echo ">> kernel=${KERNEL}" >&2

set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
  "${QEMU}" \
    -M microvm,rtc=off \
    -accel "${ACCEL}" -cpu "${CPU}" -m 256M -smp 1 \
    -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    -no-reboot \
    -serial stdio -display none 2>&1)"
RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
  echo ">> PASS: observed marker '${MARKER}'" >&2
  exit 0
fi

echo ">> FAIL: marker '${MARKER}' not seen (qemu/timeout rc=${RC})" >&2
exit 1

files.note: UPDATED from the live file on disk: MARKER -> 'M3: mmu OK' and the header comment retold for M3 (MMU bring-up from cold + Break-Before-Make remap proof; earlier milestones' markers still print in the same boot). QEMU virt/cortex-a72/-m 128M/-nographic invocation, timeout and fail-closed grep semantics unchanged.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-aarch64.sh -- QEMU `virt` runner (milestone M3: MMU).
#
# Boots the aarch64 tb-os image under QEMU, captures the PL011 serial stream,
# and asserts the executable Definition-of-Done marker "M3: mmu OK" (the
# MMU-from-cold bring-up + Break-Before-Make remap proof; M0's hello, M1's
# trap round-trip and M2's ping-pong still print earlier in the same boot, and
# "mmu-test: enabled, serial alive" right before the marker proves the Device
# mapping for the UART survived the enable).
# Doubles as the cargo runner for target aarch64-tabos-none (cargo passes the
# freshly built ELF as $1) and is runnable by hand on WSL2.
#
# Invocation sources:
#   * QEMU `virt` board: UART0 = ARM PL011 @ 0x0900_0000, FDT pointer in x0,
#     `-kernel <ELF>` enters at EL1 (QEMU docs; dtb dumped via -machine dumpdtb).
#   * `-nographic` already routes the serial onto stdio AND muxes the monitor;
#     additionally passing `-serial stdio` makes QEMU abort with
#     "cannot use stdio by multiple character devices", so we deliberately do
#     not pass both -- `-nographic` *is* the requested serial-on-stdio path.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/aarch64-tabos-none/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
QEMU="${QEMU_AARCH64:-qemu-system-aarch64}"
MARKER="M3: mmu OK"
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] error: '${QEMU}' not found on PATH" >&2
    exit 127
fi

# -M virt          : QEMU AArch64 'virt' machine (PL011 @ 0x09000000, GICv2, FDT)
# -cpu cortex-a72  : real A72; guest entry = EL1h, MMU off, DAIF masked, x0=FDT
# -m 128M          : virt RAM = 0x4000_0000..0x4800_0000; image links at +512 KiB
# -nographic       : headless; PL011 -> this terminal's stdio (see note above)
# -no-reboot       : do not loop on a fatal guest event
# -kernel <ELF>    : load PT_LOADs at p_paddr, jump to e_entry (= _start)
set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
    "${QEMU}" \
        -M virt \
        -cpu cortex-a72 \
        -m 128M \
        -nographic \
        -no-reboot \
        -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    < /dev/null 2>&1)"
QEMU_RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] PASS -- observed DoD marker: '${MARKER}'"
    exit 0
fi

echo "[run-aarch64] FAIL -- marker '${MARKER}' not seen" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]   (qemu exit=${QEMU_RC}; the kernel halts in wfi, so a" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]    ${TIMEOUT_SECS}s timeout/exit=124 is expected -- the grep is the verdict)" >&2
exit 1

issues.problem: The shared typed page-table layer does not exist on disk, and lib.rs has no `mod mmu;` declaration. Both arch backends do `use crate::mmu::PageTable512;` (arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54, arch/aarch64/mmu.rs:75), so the workspace does not compile. Verified: `cargo check -p tb-hal` fails with E0432 "unresolved import `crate::mmu`" at crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54. Every M3 alignment guarantee (CR3/TTBR0 BADDR, table pointers) hangs off this missing type's `align(4096)`.
issues.fix: Create crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs containing `#[repr(C, align(4096))] pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64; 512]);` (the field MUST be pub: both arch files construct `PageTable512([0; 512])`), plus the SHIFT_*/level_index/make_entry helpers shown in the change set, and add `mod mmu;` to crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs. The `align(4096)` attribute is load-bearing: without it the low 12 address bits of a table's address can be nonzero and would bleed into descriptor flag bits, producing the silent boot hang the review lens warns about.

issues.problem: arch/mod.rs was not updated for M3: the cfg'd re-export lists (lines 38-41 x86_64, 46-48 aarch64) still export only the M0-M2 set {breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init}. The M3 lib.rs facade (`pub fn mmu_init() { arch::mmu_init() }` / `arch::mmu_selftest()`) cannot resolve, and today the per-arch `pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};` is dead code (cargo emits `unused_imports` for arch/x86_64/mod.rs:70).
issues.fix: Add `mmu_init, mmu_selftest` to both cfg'd `pub use` lists: `pub use self::x86_64::{breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, mmu_init, mmu_selftest, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init};` and the identical change for the aarch64 arm.

issues.problem: The M3 change set is only half-applied to disk. lib.rs on disk is still the M2 version (header says "M0 serial + M1 traps + M2 tasks"; no `mod mmu;`, no public `mmu_init`/`mmu_selftest`), and kernel/src/main.rs is also still the M2 version (header "M2: cooperative context switch"; rust_main never calls mmu_init/mmu_selftest, never prints "M3: mmu OK", and lacks the BOOT_TASK_RAW/M2_PASSED hand-back-to-bootstrap machinery the M3 main.rs in the change set adds). Only the four files under crates/tb-hal/src/arch/{x86_64,aarch64}/{mmu.rs,mod.rs} were written. Even after fixing the compile errors, the M3 DoD marker can never appear.
issues.fix: Apply the M3 versions from the change set: (a) lib.rs — add `mod mmu;` plus the safe facade `pub fn mmu_init() { arch::mmu_init() }` and `pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool { arch::mmu_selftest() }` with the M3 docs; (b) kernel/src/main.rs — the M3 version where task A yields back to the bootstrap context after the M2 verdict and rust_main then runs `tb_hal::mmu_init()`, prints "mmu-test: enabled, serial alive", and prints "M3: mmu OK" / "M3: FAIL" on `tb_hal::mmu_selftest()`'s bool. Also affected: C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/kernel/src/main.rs.

issues.problem: Both runner scripts on disk still gate on the M2 marker: `MARKER='M2: context-switch OK'` (run-x86_64.sh:24, run-aarch64.sh:24). The M3 script versions in the change set (MARKER 'M3: mmu OK') were never written, so the fail-closed DoD gate validates the wrong milestone — a silently broken/hanging mmu_selftest would still PASS the runner as long as M2 prints.
issues.fix: Apply the M3 script versions from the change set: set MARKER='M3: mmu OK' in both scripts/run-x86_64.sh and scripts/run-aarch64.sh (and the updated header comments). Also affected: C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/scripts/run-aarch64.sh.

issues.problem: mmu_init enables SCTLR_EL1.{M,C,I} from cold without first invalidating the TLB (`tlbi vmalle1`) or the D/I caches, whose contents are architecturally UNKNOWN out of reset. Harmless on the only supported runner today (QEMU TCG models neither TLBs-with-stale-state nor caches), but it becomes a real stale-translation/stale-line hazard the moment the aarch64 runner gains KVM (the x86 script already opportunistically uses KVM) or real hardware.
issues.fix: In mmu_init, before step (5), add a one-time cold invalidation: `tlbi vmalle1` -> `dsb nsh` -> `isb` (and ideally `ic iallu` + `isb` before setting SCTLR_EL1.I). This is the same sequence Linux's head.S performs before enabling the MMU; it costs three instructions and removes the QEMU-TCG-only assumption.

reviews.verdict: The MMU logic itself survives the adversarial trace on both architectures — but the change set is half-applied and the workspace does not compile, which is a hard blocker. Bit-level audit (all verified against the on-disk arch files): aarch64 cold bring-up order is exactly right (volatile table writes with MMU off -> dsb ishst -> msr mair/tcr/ttbr0 -> isb -> mrs/orr SCTLR M|C|I -> isb); the L1[0] Device 1 GiB block (0x701) covers the PL011; AF (bit 10) is set on every leaf descriptor written (BLOCK_DEVICE/BLOCK_NORMAL/PAGE_NORMAL all include 1<<10); descriptor low bits are correct (L1 block 0b01, L3 page 0b11, table 0b11); TCR_EL1 independently recomputes to 0x2_0099_3519 (T0SZ=25, TG0=0, EPD1=1, IPS=0b010); the BBM remap follows the exact required order (invalid -> dsb ishst -> tlbi vaae1is VA>>12 -> dsb ish -> isb -> new -> dsb ishst). x86: VA 0x4000_0000 decomposes to PML4[0]/PDPT[1]/PD[0]/PT[0] and the walk/splice indexes the chain correctly against the boot hierarchy (boot.rs confirms PDPT[1] is vacant and .bss is zeroed); all entries are P|RW; invlpg is present after the present-leaf remap; statics get 4096 alignment from PageTable512; and the no-CR3-reload claim is architecturally correct. However: crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs (the shared PageTable512 layer both backends import) does not exist on disk, lib.rs and kernel/src/main.rs are still the M2 versions (no `mod mmu;`, no facade, no caller, no \"M3: mmu OK\"), arch/mod.rs never re-exports mmu_init/mmu_selftest, and both runner scripts still grep the M2 marker. `cargo check -p tb-hal` fails today with E0432 unresolved import `crate::mmu`. Fix the four integration gaps (write the shared mmu.rs with the load-bearing `#[repr(C, align(4096))]`, add the re-exports, apply the M3 lib.rs/main.rs/scripts) and this milestone should pass as designed; the only substantive hardware-correctness note is the missing cold TLB/cache invalidation before SCTLR enable on aarch64, which is benign under QEMU TCG but should be added (tlbi vmalle1; dsb nsh; isb) before KVM or real hardware enter the picture.

issues.problem: The shared typed page-table layer shown in the diff was never written to the tree, and the on-disk lib.rs is still the M2 version with no `mod mmu;` declaration. Both new arch backends import it (`use crate::mmu::PageTable512;` at arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54 and arch/aarch64/mmu.rs:75), so the whole workspace fails to compile on both targets. Reproduced: `cargo check -p tb-hal` -> error[E0432]: unresolved import `crate::mmu` (crates\tb-hal\src\arch\x86_64\mmu.rs:54:12; the aarch64 arm hits the identical error at line 75). Even with `mod mmu;` added, a missing file would be E0583.
issues.fix: Create crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs and add `mod mmu;` to crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs (the diff's M3 lib.rs already carries it). The file MUST define `#[repr(C, align(4096))] pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64; 512]);` with the tuple field `pub` — both arch backends construct `PageTable512([0; 512])` from foreign modules, so a private field is a further compile error (E0423 'cannot initialize a tuple struct which contains private fields'); the diff hunk truncates before the type definition, so verify this explicitly. Machine-verified: with exactly that definition present, the entire x86_64 arm of tb-hal (all asm!, Cell4K, statics, mmu_init/mmu_selftest) compiles with zero errors and zero warnings.

issues.problem: This file is not part of the M3 changeset at all, and its two cfg'd re-export lists still carry only the M0-M2 set: `pub use self::x86_64::{breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init};` (same for aarch64) — no `mmu_init`/`mmu_selftest`. Both per-arch mod.rs files explicitly state the M3 surface is 're-exported through arch/mod.rs so lib.rs can expose tb_hal::mmu_init', so the moment the diff's M3 lib.rs facade lands, the build breaks. Reproduced in a scratch build: error[E0425]: cannot find function `mmu_init` in module `arch` and error[E0425]: cannot find function `mmu_selftest` in module `arch`. Until then the per-arch `pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};` is dead (unused_imports warning, already visible in cargo check output).
issues.fix: Add `mmu_init, mmu_selftest` to BOTH cfg'd re-export lists in crates/tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs: `pub use self::x86_64::{breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, mmu_init, mmu_selftest, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init};` and the identical aarch64 line; also extend the INTERNAL CONTRACT doc list with `pub fn mmu_init();` and `pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool;`. Verified: with this edit plus the lib.rs facade, tb-hal compiles cleanly.

issues.problem: The M3 changeset only half-landed: `git status` shows just 4 files (modified arch/{x86_64,aarch64}/mod.rs, new arch/{x86_64,aarch64}/mmu.rs); the M3 versions of crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs, kernel/src/main.rs, scripts/run-x86_64.sh and scripts/run-aarch64.sh shown in the diff were never written. On disk, main.rs is still the M2 file (header 'M2: cooperative context switch', no mmu_init/mmu_selftest calls, no BOOT_TASK_RAW/M2_PASSED hand-back) and both scripts still grep 'M2: context-switch OK'. If the reviewed scripts land (MARKER='M3: mmu OK') without the M3 main.rs — or in any partial order — both QEMU runners fail-closed on both arches because the kernel never prints the M3 marker, even after the compile errors above are fixed.
issues.fix: Land the remaining five files atomically in one commit: the M3 lib.rs (`mod mmu;` + the `pub fn mmu_init()`/`pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool` facade delegating to `arch::`), the M3 kernel/src/main.rs (keeps M0 hello -> M1 breakpoint round-trip -> M2 ping-pong, then task A yields back to the bootstrap context, gates on M2_PASSED, runs mmu_init + 'mmu-test: enabled, serial alive' + mmu_selftest -> 'M3: mmu OK'), and both M3 run scripts. Gate the commit on scripts/run-x86_64.sh and scripts/run-aarch64.sh both passing, so the marker and the kernel can never diverge again.

reviews.verdict: REQUEST CHANGES — the new MMU code itself is compile-clean and regression-safe, but the changeset is incomplete and the tree does not build. Verified findings: (1) the workspace currently fails `cargo check -p tb-hal` with error[E0432] unresolved import `crate::mmu` (arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54, arch/aarch64/mmu.rs:75) because crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs was never created and the on-disk lib.rs is still the M2 version without `mod mmu;`; (2) crates/tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs is missing from the changeset and never re-exports mmu_init/mmu_selftest, producing error[E0425] x2 the moment the M3 lib.rs facade lands (reproduced in a scratch build); (3) kernel/src/main.rs and both run scripts on disk are still M2, so the diff's 'M3: mmu OK' runners would fail-closed on both arches even after the build is fixed. Everything else under the lens checks out: both per-arch mod.rs keep every prior module and re-export; the diff's main.rs runs the M1 trap test and M2 ping-pong verbatim before M3; the kernel crate still contains zero unsafe blocks (the documented no-forbid exception for #[unsafe(no_mangle)] is unchanged); all asm!/operand syntax is valid — the full x86_64 arm was machine-verified to compile with zero errors/warnings once a correct PageTable512 (`#[repr(C, align(4096))]`, pub tuple field) exists, and the aarch64 asm was manually reviewed (no aarch64 core sysroot available locally); table statics inherit 4096 alignment transitively through the repr(C)/repr(transparent) cell wrappers; no name collisions (crate::mmu vs arch::*::mmu are distinct paths; aarch64's local Frame4K never clashes since only PageTable512 is imported). Fixes: write src/mmu.rs with `pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64; 512])`, add the two-symbol re-export to arch/mod.rs, and land lib.rs + main.rs + both scripts atomically, gated on both QEMU runners.

issues.problem: The shared typed page-table layer does not exist on disk, and lib.rs has no `mod mmu;` declaration. Both arch backends do `use crate::mmu::PageTable512;` (arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54, arch/aarch64/mmu.rs:75), so the workspace does not compile. Verified: `cargo check -p tb-hal` fails with E0432 "unresolved import `crate::mmu`" at crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54. Every M3 alignment guarantee (CR3/TTBR0 BADDR, table pointers) hangs off this missing type's `align(4096)`.
issues.fix: Create crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs containing `#[repr(C, align(4096))] pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64; 512]);` (the field MUST be pub: both arch files construct `PageTable512([0; 512])`), plus the SHIFT_*/level_index/make_entry helpers shown in the change set, and add `mod mmu;` to crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs. The `align(4096)` attribute is load-bearing: without it the low 12 address bits of a table's address can be nonzero and would bleed into descriptor flag bits, producing the silent boot hang the review lens warns about.

issues.problem: arch/mod.rs was not updated for M3: the cfg'd re-export lists (lines 38-41 x86_64, 46-48 aarch64) still export only the M0-M2 set {breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init}. The M3 lib.rs facade (`pub fn mmu_init() { arch::mmu_init() }` / `arch::mmu_selftest()`) cannot resolve, and today the per-arch `pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};` is dead code (cargo emits `unused_imports` for arch/x86_64/mod.rs:70).
issues.fix: Add `mmu_init, mmu_selftest` to both cfg'd `pub use` lists: `pub use self::x86_64::{breakpoint, ctx_switch, halt, install_traps, mmu_init, mmu_selftest, serial_init, serial_write_byte, task_stack_init};` and the identical change for the aarch64 arm.

issues.problem: The M3 change set is only half-applied to disk. lib.rs on disk is still the M2 version (header says "M0 serial + M1 traps + M2 tasks"; no `mod mmu;`, no public `mmu_init`/`mmu_selftest`), and kernel/src/main.rs is also still the M2 version (header "M2: cooperative context switch"; rust_main never calls mmu_init/mmu_selftest, never prints "M3: mmu OK", and lacks the BOOT_TASK_RAW/M2_PASSED hand-back-to-bootstrap machinery the M3 main.rs in the change set adds). Only the four files under crates/tb-hal/src/arch/{x86_64,aarch64}/{mmu.rs,mod.rs} were written. Even after fixing the compile errors, the M3 DoD marker can never appear.
issues.fix: Apply the M3 versions from the change set: (a) lib.rs — add `mod mmu;` plus the safe facade `pub fn mmu_init() { arch::mmu_init() }` and `pub fn mmu_selftest() -> bool { arch::mmu_selftest() }` with the M3 docs; (b) kernel/src/main.rs — the M3 version where task A yields back to the bootstrap context after the M2 verdict and rust_main then runs `tb_hal::mmu_init()`, prints "mmu-test: enabled, serial alive", and prints "M3: mmu OK" / "M3: FAIL" on `tb_hal::mmu_selftest()`'s bool. Also affected: C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/kernel/src/main.rs.

issues.problem: Both runner scripts on disk still gate on the M2 marker: `MARKER='M2: context-switch OK'` (run-x86_64.sh:24, run-aarch64.sh:24). The M3 script versions in the change set (MARKER 'M3: mmu OK') were never written, so the fail-closed DoD gate validates the wrong milestone — a silently broken/hanging mmu_selftest would still PASS the runner as long as M2 prints.
issues.fix: Apply the M3 script versions from the change set: set MARKER='M3: mmu OK' in both scripts/run-x86_64.sh and scripts/run-aarch64.sh (and the updated header comments). Also affected: C:/Users/Arda/workspaces/@cogitave/tabos/scripts/run-aarch64.sh.

issues.problem: mmu_init enables SCTLR_EL1.{M,C,I} from cold without first invalidating the TLB (`tlbi vmalle1`) or the D/I caches, whose contents are architecturally UNKNOWN out of reset. Harmless on the only supported runner today (QEMU TCG models neither TLBs-with-stale-state nor caches), but it becomes a real stale-translation/stale-line hazard the moment the aarch64 runner gains KVM (the x86 script already opportunistically uses KVM) or real hardware.
issues.fix: In mmu_init, before step (5), add a one-time cold invalidation: `tlbi vmalle1` -> `dsb nsh` -> `isb` (and ideally `ic iallu` + `isb` before setting SCTLR_EL1.I). This is the same sequence Linux's head.S performs before enabling the MMU; it costs three instructions and removes the QEMU-TCG-only assumption.

issues.problem: The shared typed page-table layer shown in the diff was never written to the tree, and the on-disk lib.rs is still the M2 version with no `mod mmu;` declaration. Both new arch backends import it (`use crate::mmu::PageTable512;` at arch/x86_64/mmu.rs:54 and arch/aarch64/mmu.rs:75), so the whole workspace fails to compile on both targets. Reproduced: `cargo check -p tb-hal` -> error[E0432]: unresolved import `crate::mmu` (crates\tb-hal\src\arch\x86_64\mmu.rs:54:12; the aarch64 arm hits the identical error at line 75). Even with `mod mmu;` added, a missing file would be E0583.
issues.fix: Create crates/tb-hal/src/mmu.rs and add `mod mmu;` to crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs (the diff's M3 lib.rs already carries it). The file MUST define `#[repr(C, align(4096))] pub struct PageTable512(pub [u64; 512]);` with the tuple field `pub` — both arch backends construct `PageTable512([0; 512])` from foreign modules, so a private field is a further compile error (E0423 'cannot initialize a tuple struct which contains private fields'); the diff hunk truncates before the type definition, so verify this explicitly. Machine-verified: with exactly that definition present, the entire x86_64 arm of tb-hal (all asm!, Cell4K, statics, mmu_init/mmu_selftest) compiles with zero errors and zero warnings.
